title: Pyrus honanensis
intro: Pyrus honanensis, also known as the Korean pear tree, is a deciduous tree species belonging to the Rosaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its use in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of fever. Pyrus honanensis contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pyrus honanensis include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support immune function and overall well-being.

parts: Pyrus honanensis. The medicinal parts of Pyrus honanensis are primarily its fruits, leaves, and bark, which have been used in traditional Chinese medicine for various purposes.
